version: 2.0.1
applies: remix-i18next@8 & react-router@8
target: rules
domain: i18n
paths: ["**/i18n*", "**/entry.server*", "**/entry.client*", "**/middleware/i18n*"]
tags: [i18n, routing, ssr, remix-i18next, middleware, setup, configuration, server]
---

# React Router 8 i18n — Setup & Configuration

Internationalization for React Router 8 SSR apps using **remix-i18next v8** (middleware-based), i18next, and react-i18next.

## Documentation

| Source | URL | Notes |
|--------|-----|-------|
| remix-i18next | https://github.com/sergiodxa/remix-i18next | v8 uses `createI18nextMiddleware` |
| i18next docs | https://www.i18next.com/overview/getting-started | Core i18n library |
| react-i18next | https://react.i18next.com | React bindings |
| i18next-cli | https://github.com/i18next/i18next-cli | Key extraction + type generation |
| Context7 | `/i18next/i18next` | Good coverage |

## What Changed in v8

- **remix-i18next v8 is middleware-based.** `createI18nextMiddleware` replaces the old `new RemixI18Next(...)` server instance. It returns `[middleware, getLocale, getInstance]`.
- **Locale detection runs in RR8 route middleware**, not in `entry.server.tsx`. Middleware is on by default in v8 — no `future` flag.
- **`useChangeLanguage` was removed.** Sync the language with a `useEffect` (see `VendorReactRouter8I18nUsage`).
- **Resources are bundled TS modules** and served to the browser by a resource route + `i18next-fetch-backend` — no `i18next-fs-backend` / `i18next-http-backend`.

> **Pitfall — v8 subpath exports dropped:** remix-i18next v8 exposes only the `.` entry. Old imports like `remix-i18next/middleware` or `remix-i18next/react` no longer resolve — import `createI18nextMiddleware` from the bare `'remix-i18next'`.

> **Pitfall — `findLocale` param is now an args object, not a bare `Request`:** in v8 it receives `LanguageDetectorArgs` (RR8 `MiddlewareArgs`, which has both `request` and a normalized `url`). Writing `async findLocale(request)` errors TS7006 (implicit any). Destructure the field you need: `async findLocale({ url })` for path-based detection, `async findLocale({ request })` for cookie / `Accept-Language`.

## Dependencies

```json
{
  "dependencies": {
    "i18next": "^26.0.0",
    "i18next-browser-languagedetector": "^8.2.0",
    "i18next-fetch-backend": "^7.0.0",
    "react-i18next": "^17.0.0",
    "remix-i18next": "^8.0.0"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "i18next-cli": "^1.65.0"
  }
}
```

## File Structure

```
app/
├── i18n-config.ts            # supportedLngs + bundled resources
├── entry.server.tsx          # SSR entry — uses getInstance(context)
├── entry.client.tsx          # Client hydration — fetch backend
├── root.tsx                  # Registers middleware + syncs language
├── middleware/
│   └── i18next.ts            # createI18nextMiddleware (detection)
├── lib/cookies/
│   └── i18next-cookie.server.ts
├── locales/
│   ├── en.ts                 # barrel: { common } as const
│   ├── en/common.json        # actual strings
│   ├── de.ts
│   └── de/common.json
├── routes/
│   ├── _lang-guard.tsx       # layout route: 404 on unsupported :lang
│   └── api/locales.ts        # serves resources[lang][ns] to the client
└── types/i18next.d.ts        # generated by i18next-cli
i18next.config.ts             # extraction + type config
```

## Language Config & Bundled Resources

`app/locales/en.ts` (one barrel per language):

```typescript
import common from './en/common.json'

const resources = { common } as const
export default resources
```

`app/i18n-config.ts`:

```typescript
import de from '~/locales/de'
import en from '~/locales/en'

export const supportedLngs = ['en', 'de'] as const
export type Locale = (typeof supportedLngs)[number]
export const defaultLocale: Locale = 'en'

export const i18nConfig = {
  defaultNS: 'common',
  fallbackLng: defaultLocale as string,
  resources: { de, en },
  supportedLngs: [...supportedLngs] as string[],
}
```

## Locale Cookie

```typescript
// app/lib/cookies/i18next-cookie.server.ts
import { createCookie } from 'react-router'

export const localeCookie = createCookie('lng', {
  httpOnly: true,
  maxAge: 60 * 60 * 24 * 365, // 1 year — persist explicit language choice
  path: '/',
  sameSite: 'lax',
  secure: process.env.NODE_ENV === 'production',
})
```

## Detection Middleware (the core of v8 i18n)

```typescript
// app/middleware/i18next.ts
import { createI18nextMiddleware } from 'remix-i18next'

import { i18nConfig } from '~/i18n-config'
import { localeCookie } from '~/lib/cookies/i18next-cookie.server'

export const [i18nextMiddleware, getLocale, getInstance] = createI18nextMiddleware({
  detection: {
    cookie: localeCookie,
    fallbackLanguage: i18nConfig.fallbackLng,
    async findLocale({ url }) {
      // RR8 hands middleware a NORMALIZED url — the `.data` suffix RR adds to
      // data requests is already stripped, so path detection works on client
      // revalidations without manual stripping.
      const first = url.pathname.split('/', 2).at(1) || ''
      return i18nConfig.supportedLngs.includes(first) ? first : null
    },
    // Cookie MUST come before header so an explicit switch survives visits to
    // non-prefixed URLs.
    order: ['custom', 'searchParams', 'cookie', 'header'],
    supportedLanguages: i18nConfig.supportedLngs,
  },
  i18next: {
    defaultNS: i18nConfig.defaultNS,
    resources: i18nConfig.resources,
  },
})
```

## Register Middleware in root.tsx

Middleware is enabled by default in RR8 — just export it. Read the locale from `context` (not `request`):

```typescript
import { getLocale, i18nextMiddleware } from '~/middleware/i18next'
import { localeCookie } from '~/lib/cookies/i18next-cookie.server'

export const middleware = [i18nextMiddleware]

export const loader = async ({ context }: Route.LoaderArgs) => {
  const locale = getLocale(context)
  const headers = new Headers()
  headers.append('Set-Cookie', await localeCookie.serialize(locale))
  return data({ locale }, { headers })
}
```

## SSR Entry

Use the middleware's `getInstance(context)` — no manual `createInstance()`/backend wiring:

```tsx
// app/entry.server.tsx (abridged)
import { I18nextProvider } from 'react-i18next'
import { type EntryContext, type RouterContextProvider, ServerRouter } from 'react-router'

import { getInstance } from './middleware/i18next'

export default function handleRequest(
  request: Request,
  responseStatusCode: number,
  responseHeaders: Headers,
  entryContext: EntryContext,
  routerContext: RouterContextProvider,
) {
  // ...renderToPipeableStream(...)
  return (
    <I18nextProvider i18n={getInstance(routerContext)}>
      <ServerRouter context={entryContext} url={request.url} />
    </I18nextProvider>
  )
}
```

## Client Entry

The browser loads namespaces from the resource route via `i18next-fetch-backend`:

```typescript
// app/entry.client.tsx
import i18next from 'i18next'
import I18nextBrowserLanguageDetector from 'i18next-browser-languagedetector'
import Fetch from 'i18next-fetch-backend'
import { startTransition, StrictMode } from 'react'
import { hydrateRoot } from 'react-dom/client'
import { I18nextProvider, initReactI18next } from 'react-i18next'
import { HydratedRouter } from 'react-router/dom'

import { i18nConfig } from '~/i18n-config'

await i18next
  .use(initReactI18next)
  .use(Fetch)
  .use(I18nextBrowserLanguageDetector)
  .init({
    backend: { loadPath: '/api/locales/{{lng}}/{{ns}}' },
    defaultNS: i18nConfig.defaultNS,
    detection: { caches: [], order: ['htmlTag'] }, // trust server-set <html lang>
    fallbackLng: i18nConfig.fallbackLng,
    ns: [i18nConfig.defaultNS],
    supportedLngs: i18nConfig.supportedLngs,
  })

startTransition(() => {
  hydrateRoot(
    document,
    <I18nextProvider i18n={i18next}>
      <StrictMode>
        <HydratedRouter />
      </StrictMode>
    </I18nextProvider>,
  )
})
```

## Locale Resource Route

Serves one namespace per request; the server already has every locale bundled:

```typescript
// app/routes/api/locales.ts  (routes.ts: route('api/locales/:lang/:ns', ...))
import { data } from 'react-router'

import { i18nConfig, supportedLngs } from '~/i18n-config'
import { type Route } from './+types/locales'

export function loader({ params }: Route.LoaderArgs) {
  const lang = params.lang as (typeof supportedLngs)[number]
  if (!supportedLngs.includes(lang)) return data({ error: 'bad language' }, { status: 400 })

  const namespaces = i18nConfig.resources[lang]
  const ns = params.ns
  if (!ns || !(ns in namespaces)) return data({ error: 'bad namespace' }, { status: 400 })

  // Cache in production (CDN + browser) — locales change rarely.
  return data(namespaces[ns as keyof typeof namespaces])
}
```

## Language Guard

Validate `:lang` once in a layout route instead of in every child loader:

```tsx
// app/routes/_lang-guard.tsx  (routes.ts: prefix(':lang', [ layout('./routes/_lang-guard.tsx', [...]) ]))
import { data, Outlet } from 'react-router'

import { i18nConfig } from '~/i18n-config'
import { type Route } from './+types/_lang-guard'

export function loader({ params }: Route.LoaderArgs) {
  if (!params.lang || !i18nConfig.supportedLngs.includes(params.lang)) {
    throw data(null, { status: 404, statusText: 'Not Found' })
  }
}

export default function LangGuard() {
  return <Outlet />
}
```
